property.audit_notes: Median home value is plausible for downtown condos in 1990, though higher than tract median. Commercial vacancy reflects post-S&L crisis recovery.
Beginning of dot-com boom influence on downtown property values.
Peak of dot-com bubble. Commercial vacancy starts to rise.
Dot-com bust impact: home value growth stalls, commercial vacancy doubles.
Impact of the 2008 financial crisis begins to show.
Value aligns well with ACS 2006-2010 median for Tract 11 ($380k).
COVID-19 pandemic impact: commercial vacancy rises, but residential values continue to climb.
Large year-over-year increase reflects the post-pandemic real estate boom in Austin.
Slight market correction in home values, consistent with Austin-wide trends in 2023.
Future projections are inherently low confidence.
Future projections are inherently low confidence.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: CORRECTED median_household_income from $48,000 to $35,000. Original value was implausibly high for 1990, exceeding Austin MSA median (~$29k). Corrected value is a more reasonable estimate for a professional downtown population. Employment sectors data is highly plausible.
CORRECTED median_household_income from $56,000 to $45,000 based on interpolation between corrected 1990 and 2000 values. Original value was too high.

CORRECTED median_household_income from $68,000 to $55,000. Original value was implausibly high compared to Austin MSA median (~$48k). Corrected value is a more reasonable estimate for a professional downtown population.
CORRECTED median_household_income from $75,000 to $60,000 based on interpolation between corrected 2000 and 2010 values. Original value was too high.

CORRECTED median_household_income from $82,000 to $65,000. Original value was implausibly high compared to Austin MSA median (~$59k). Corrected value is a more reasonable estimate for a professional downtown population. Unemployment rate reflects Great Recession impact.
CORRECTED median_household_income from $98,000 to $78,000. Original value was implausibly high compared to Austin MSA median (~$71k).

CORRECTED median_household_income from $115,000 to $90,000. Original value was implausibly high compared to Austin MSA median (~$85k). Unemployment and eviction rates reflect COVID-19 pandemic impacts.
CORRECTED median_household_income from $135,000 to $110,000. Original value was an overly aggressive projection. All 2025 data are projections and have low confidence.
